task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Client Entertainment' expense category limit is updated to $100.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sentrifugo Expense Categories page. The 'Client Entertainment' category should have its limit explicitly set to 100.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Telecom & Internet' expense category is discontinued.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sentrifugo Expense Categories page. The 'Telecom & Internet' category should be marked as inactive or removed from the active categories list.

task_completion.description: Verify that David Kim's $85 Client Entertainment claim is Approved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sentrifugo expense claims list or adjudication page for David Kim. The claim for $85 under 'Client Entertainment' must show a status of 'Approved'.

task_completion.description: Verify that David Kim's $120 Client Entertainment claim is Rejected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sentrifugo expense claims list or adjudication page for David Kim. The claim for $120 under 'Client Entertainment' must show a status of 'Rejected'.

task_completion.description: Verify that David Kim's $45 Telecom & Internet claim is Rejected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Sentrifugo expense claims list or adjudication page for David Kim. The claim for $45 under 'Telecom & Internet' must show a status of 'Rejected'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the web browser to interact with Sentrifugo.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Sentrifugo application being accessed and modified through a web browser interface.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use direct database commands to update the records.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing MySQL or other database commands used to alter expense categories or claim statuses.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent read the austerity memo.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the q3_austerity_memo.txt file opened in a text editor or its contents displayed in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The austerity memo dictates that the 'Client Entertainment' expense limit is reduced to $100.00 and the 'Telecom & Internet' category is discontinued. For David Kim (EMP005), there are three claims: an $85 Client Entertainment claim (must be Approved), a $120 Client Entertainment claim (must be Rejected), and a $45 Telecom & Internet claim (must be Rejected).
